steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between an independent clause (manuscripts surviving to the present day) and a gerund phrase (being passed down...). The question tests which punctuation — or lack of it — correctly connects the clause to the prepositional phrase that explains how the manuscripts survived.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — period: creates a sentence fragment — 'being passed down...' cannot stand alone. ✗
  • Choice B — no punctuation: lets 'by being passed down' attach directly to the clause as a prepositional phrase of means — no punctuation needed. ✓
  • Choice C — comma after 'by': inserting a comma between a preposition and its object is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice D — semicolon: like the period, creates a fragment after the semicolon. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
